title: ochrosia acuminata
intro: Ochrosia acuminata, also known as the "Gamboge tree," is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, its bark containing a bright yellow resin used as a dye and in traditional medicine. The resin of Ochrosia acuminata has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions, such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Ochrosia acuminata resin is commonly used in herbal preparations, including capsules, tablets, and teas, and is also used as a natural food coloring.
